summary.intent: Add GitHub Issues and Discussions links with icons to the web UI header, including live count badges fetched from the GitHub API and cached locally.
summary.outcome: Implemented header links with GitHub Octicon SVG icons, count badges with localStorage caching (1hr TTL), and fixed two CI failures (biome formatting and file size limit) before merging via PR #806.

repo: Icon masks are SVG files stored in crates/web/src/assets/icons/masks/, referenced as url('./icons/masks/...') in compiled style.css (relative to crates/web/src/assets/), not relative to input.css source.
Tailwind is built via crates/web/ui/build.sh, outputting to crates/web/src/assets/css/style.css. A legacy style.css path also exists and can receive stale rebuild artifacts.
The frontend module pattern uses direct document.getElementById wiring at load time in app.tsx; new modules are imported there.
CI uses biome 2.4.6 but the local install was 0.3.3 — formatting output differs between versions, causing a single biome error on github-stats.ts.
scripts/check-file-size.sh enforces a 1500-line limit per file; crates/tools/src/skill_tools.rs at 1557 lines was pre-existing and needed an allowlist entry.

code.finding: New module: fetches open issues via GitHub Search API and discussions via Discussions REST API in parallel, caches both in localStorage under a single key with 1hr TTL, updates DOM badge elements.
Added .header-badge class — always visible (inline-block), small pill style; no hide/show toggling needed since badge is always shown even when count is unavailable.
New .icon-github-issue and .icon-github-discussion classes follow the existing mask-image CSS pattern used by other icon-link classes.
Issues and Discussions links each contain a <span class='header-badge'> for count injection by github-stats.ts.

workflow: Run biome with the exact CI version (npx @biomejs/biome@2.4.6) before committing to catch formatting discrepancies invisible to older local installs.

GitHub Discussions count is NOT available via REST API without auth; only the GraphQL API exposes it. Issues count via Search API (/search/issues?q=repo:...+type:issue+state:open) works unauthenticated.
When biome ci reports 1 error among hundreds of warnings, grep for 'error' lines to isolate the actionable failure rather than chasing warnings.

friction: Local biome version (0.3.3) vs CI biome version (2.4.6) mismatch caused a silent formatting divergence that only surfaced in CI, requiring a separate fix commit.
Icon CSS path resolution was non-obvious: url() in input.css resolves relative to the compiled output location (src/assets/), not relative to input.css itself.
skill_tools.rs file size violation was pre-existing tech debt that blocked CI and had to be fixed before the feature branch could land cleanly.
Determining the single actual biome error among 601 warnings required multiple bash invocations due to ANSI escape codes interfering with grep output.
GitHub Discussions REST API endpoint may 404/403 without auth even for public repos — had to fall back to graceful degradation.
open_items: crates/tools/src/skill_tools.rs was added to the file size allowlist rather than refactored — the underlying overgrowth issue remains unaddressed.
601 biome warnings across the codebase (e2e specs, tool-helpers.ts, etc.) are suppressed but not fixed; they may eventually be promoted to errors on biome upgrades.
Discussions count fetch is unauthenticated and may silently fail if GitHub changes REST API auth requirements — a backend proxy with a stored PAT would be more robust.
Issues count via Search API includes PRs in some configurations — may need to verify the query correctly excludes pull requests.
